title: Bangladesh e-Passport Application Form (New/Re-Issue) (Department of Immigration and Passports)
description: The Department of Immigration and Passports' (DIP) "e-Passport Application Form (New/Re-Issue)", the offline/embassy-submission specimen distributed via the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' (MOFA) forms portal for applicants who cannot or do not use the online e-passport portal (epassport.gov.bd). Opens Bangladesh's Passport vertical (3 of 6, alongside the already-published Taxes vertical (bd/nbr/individual-income-tax-return-form-it-11ga) and DMV vertical (bd/brta/motor-vehicle-registration-application)) and reverses a prior dead-end verdict: GOV-2591's cycle screened Bangladesh's Passport vertical and found it weak/dead-end (an online-portal-only e-passport process with no fillable specimen located at the time); this cycle found MOFA's own forms portal hosts a genuine, unauthenticated, interactive AcroForm PDF for exactly this process. A genuine 3-page, 111-AcroForm-widget specimen (independently confirmed via pdfjs-dist), bilingual (Bengali primary, English secondary) on every label. This is a combined New/Re-Issue form; this v1.0.0 scopes to the first-time (new) applicant pathway. Page 1's items 1-3 (Online Registration ID/Payment Reference/Payment Amount) are confirmed, from the PDF's own embedded default field values ("OFFLINE APPLICATION", "N/A", "N/A"), to be online-system-populated and inapplicable to this offline specimen, so they are excluded as non-applicant-entered. Item 4 (Passport Office/Bangladesh Mission) through item 8 (Type of Delivery) are modeled. Items 9-12 (Previous Passport No./Date of Issue/Date of Expiry/Reason for Re-Issue) are re-issue-only and out of scope. Personal Information (items 13-31), the Permanent Address (38-43), Present Address (44-50), Parental Information (51-58), Guardian Information (59-62, modeled as unconditionally optional: the form's own item 59 label reads "Guardian's Name – if applicable" and its page-3 declaration signature line reads "Signature of Applicant/Guardian (if Applicant is minor)", implying the guardian block applies when the applicant is a minor, but the form provides no dedicated minor/adult boolean field to gate a requiredWhen condition against), Spouse Information (63-66), and Emergency Contact (67-77) blocks are all modeled in full. Items 32-37 ("Additional Information for Official and Diplomatic Passport") are out of scope, as is items 78-79 (Pre-Police Clearance, itself printed "if applicable" with no gating field disclosed by the source) and items 80-83 (Information for Lost/Stolen Passports, a re-issue-only scenario that cannot apply to a first-time applicant). Items 84-87 (Bank Payment Information: Bank Name, Payment Reference No., Payment Date, Paid Amount) are, on independent inspection, ordinary fillable AcroForm widgets with no "for office use" heading anywhere near them (unlike this registry's bd/brta schema's office-only intake box), consistent with Bangladesh's common offline practice of paying the passport fee via a bank challan and citing that receipt's own particulars on the application; they are therefore modeled as applicant-supplied fields, not excluded as a receipt artifact. The List of Attachments checklist (10 checkboxes) is modeled for the 4 checkboxes applicable to a first-time applicant (National ID photocopy, Birth Registration Certificate photocopy, Marriage Certificate photocopy, Other); the remaining 6 (Previous Passport photocopy, NOC, GO, Retirement Document, Pre-Police Clearance Certificate, G.D.) are tied to re-issue or official/diplomatic-passport scenarios and out of scope. The Declaration (page 3) is modeled as an attestation document plus a companion declarationDate field (a genuine, if unusually positioned, AcroForm text widget found near the signature block, disclosed as a judgment call in VERIFICATION.md). A significant data-quality finding from this cycle's own independent extraction: the PDF's internal AcroForm field *names* (mostly plain numeric strings) do **not** reliably correspond to the form's own printed item numbers — e.g. printed items 9/10/11/12 carry internal field names "9"/"11"/"12"/"10" respectively — so every field in this schema was identified by its on-page position and printed label, cross-checked against the form's own text layer, never by trusting the internal PDF field name. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Bangladesh or the Department of Immigration and Passports.

source.documentRef: "e-Passport Application Form (New/Re-Issue)", Department of Immigration and Passports (footer: "Government of the People's Republic of Bangladesh, Department of Immigration and Passports, www.dip.gov.bd"), distributed for offline/embassy submission via the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' forms portal at https://mofa.gov.bd/pages/forms/69959494fcf25ca2d1011a0e

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2666. Opens Bangladesh's Passport vertical (3 of 6), reversing GOV-2591's prior dead-end verdict for this vertical. Independently re-fetched both cited sources this cycle via curl -sk with a Chrome-like User-Agent (both mofa.gov.bd and the oraclecloud object-storage host fail default TLS certificate verification, consistent with the task briefing's note and the same pattern already seen for BRTA/RJSC/ECS elsewhere in this registry): the MOFA landing page (HTTP 200, text/html, 72,484 bytes) and the direct PDF (HTTP 200, application/pdf, 4,114,743 bytes, sha256 477e229ac2869691f6bd2a09be01af0d7ebb22cdae0e4591db7687e6ac7dd862) — matching the task briefing's own cited size/hash exactly, independently re-derived. Parsed the downloaded PDF with pdfjs-dist@4 (legacy build): 3 pages, getFieldObjects() returned 111 distinct field names across 114 Widget annotations (a handful of named fields, e.g. Road_block_info, repeat across multiple widgets as decorative per-section "i" info-icon buttons, not data fields). getTextContent() returned a full, clean bilingual (Bengali/English) text layer across all 3 pages. Every field below was extracted by (1) reading the position-sorted text layer to recover the form's own printed item numbers, labels, and required-field asterisks, matching the task briefing's own transcription exactly field-for-field, and (2) independently correlating every AcroForm widget's page/rect coordinates against those same printed item-number rows to determine each item's actual field type (text/combobox/checkbox), discovering along the way that internal AcroForm field *names* do not reliably track the printed item numbers (see the schema's own description for the item-9-12 example). GOV-2672 correction: the v1.0.0 draft's combobox check queried a nonexistent `.options` property on pdfjs-dist's getFieldObjects() results (always null) and concluded the form's 33 combobox-typed items carry no embedded option list; independent re-extraction for GOV-2672 found the correct property is `.items`, and it is populated for 24 of them. All 24 are also `editable: true` AcroForm combo widgets (the user can type a value outside the printed list, not just pick one) and the form's four district-selector fields (districtOfBirth, permanentDistrict, presentDistrict, emergencyContactDistrict) share one 64-value Bangladesh-only district list with no catch-all, reused for present/emergency-contact fields this schema already discloses as capable of holding a foreign address — so those four are deliberately kept free-text (see each field's own description) despite carrying a real option list. The other 24 corrected fields (numberOfPages, durationOfPassport, typeOfDelivery, religion, typeOfCitizenship, maritalStatus, profession/fatherProfession/motherProfession/guardianProfession/spouseProfession, countryOfBirth/countryOfOtherCitizenship/presentCountry/emergencyContactCountry, fatherNationality/motherNationality/guardianNationality/spouseNationality, emergencyContactRelationship, contactCountryCode/emergencyContactCountryCode, passportOfficeOrMission, and the newly-added paidAmountCurrency) are now modeled as enum, each either backed by an "OTHER(S)" catch-all in the source's own list or a functionally exhaustive world country/nationality/calling-code list, consistent with this registry's existing precedent for large genuine-source enums (e.g. ph/comelec's and nl/denhaag's 247-value country enums). passportType and gender were already correctly modeled as enum pre-correction (from independent textual corroboration, not the broken combobox check), and are unchanged: passportType's own combobox default value is "ORDINARY" and the form's own "Additional Information for Official and Diplomatic Passport" section heading independently names the other two categories ("Official", "Diplomatic"); its combobox's `.items` also now confirm the same 3-value set directly. gender's own Bengali parenthetical translation literally lists the three options (নারী/ পুরুষ / অন্যান্য — "Female/Male/Other"), the same class of exception this registry already uses for bd/brta's vehicleOrTrailer. Contact-number items (30 and 76) are each genuinely two adjacent AcroForm widgets (a country-code combobox, both defaulting to "880", Bangladesh's calling code, plus a free-text number field), modeled as two fields each. Item 87 (Paid Amount) is likewise two widgets: a text amount field (paidAmount) plus an adjacent combobox the v1.0.0 draft excluded as an "unclear" blank-default companion with no distinguishing printed sub-label. The same GOV-2672 `.items` re-extraction found this companion combobox in fact carries a genuine 2-value list (BDT/USD), so it is now modeled as paidAmountCurrency. The unlabeled extra widget positioned between items 3 and 4 (internal name "88", default value "N/A") is unrelated (not a combobox) and remains excluded. Two valid conformance fixtures (0 errors each) plus 6 mutation-control fixtures (each raising exactly 1 error) are committed under conformance/bd/dip/e-passport-application-form/1.0.0/. See VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, every scoping/disclosure judgment call, and the complete item-to-field-type derivation.

fields.name: passportOfficeOrMission
fields.label: Passport Office/Bangladesh Mission
fields.description: Corrected re-extraction (see numberOfPages) shows a genuine 154-value embedded option list of DIP passport offices and Bangladesh diplomatic missions, the authoritative and DIP-controlled set of places this application can be filed. Modeled as enum, consistent with this registry's existing precedent for large but genuine government-defined location lists (e.g. ph/comelec's 247-value country enum).
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item 4.*: "Passport Office/Bangladesh Mission (পাসেপোর্ত অফিস/বাংলাদেশ মিশন)"

fields.label: Passport Type
fields.description: The combobox's own default value is "ORDINARY", and the form's own "Additional Information for Official and Diplomatic Passport" heading (items 32-37, out of scope for this v1.0.0) independently names the other two categories, giving a corroborated 3-way closed set.

fields.name: countryOfBirth
fields.label: Country of Birth
fields.description: Corrected re-extraction (see numberOfPages) shows a genuine 244-value embedded world-country list (comprehensive enough that no applicant's actual country of birth falls outside it), so modeled as enum rather than free-text, consistent with this registry's existing precedent for large country enums (e.g. ph/comelec, nl/denhaag).
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item 17.*: "Country of Birth (জন্মভূমি)"

fields.name: districtOfBirth
fields.label: District of Birth
fields.description: Rendered as a combobox. Corrected re-extraction (this cycle's fix: pdfjs-dist exposes a combobox's option list via `.items`, not `.options`, which the v1.0.0 draft checked and found null) shows this combobox does carry a genuine embedded option list — but it is a fixed 64-value list of Bangladesh's own administrative districts only, with no "other"/free-entry catch-all, and this exact field/list is reused across a scenario this schema already discloses as capable of being outside Bangladesh (see presentCountry/emergencyContactCountry, and the source's own dependent Post-Office/Police-Station JavaScript keyed to this same district list). Modeling it as a strict enum would make a legitimate present/emergency-contact address abroad, or a birth abroad, inexpressible, so — unlike this schema's other corrected combobox fields — it is deliberately kept free-text rather than enum.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item 18.*: "District of Birth (জন্ম েজলা)"

fields.name: contactCountryCode
fields.label: Contact No.: country code
fields.description: Corrected re-extraction (see numberOfPages) shows a genuine embedded list of international calling codes (226 distinct codes after de-duplication; the source list repeats a code once per country that shares it, e.g. "1" for every NANP country), comprehensive enough to cover any real applicant, so modeled as enum.

fields.name: contactNumber
fields.label: Contact No.: number

Modeling it as a strict enum would make a legitimate present/emergency-contact address abroad, or a birth abroad, inexpressible, so — unlike this schema's other corrected combobox fields — it is deliberately kept free-text rather than enum.

fields.name: emergencyContactDistrict
fields.label: Emergency Contact: District
fields.description: Rendered as a combobox. Corrected re-extraction (this cycle's fix: pdfjs-dist exposes a combobox's option list via `.items`, not `.options`, which the v1.0.0 draft checked and found null) shows this combobox does carry a genuine embedded option list — but it is a fixed 64-value list of Bangladesh's own administrative districts only, with no "other"/free-entry catch-all, and this exact field/list is reused across a scenario this schema already discloses as capable of being outside Bangladesh (see presentCountry/emergencyContactCountry, and the source's own dependent Post-Office/Police-Station JavaScript keyed to this same district list). Modeling it as a strict enum would make a legitimate present/emergency-contact address abroad, or a birth abroad, inexpressible, so — unlike this schema's other corrected combobox fields — it is deliberately kept free-text rather than enum.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, item 70.*: "District (েজলা)" — Emergency Contact

fields.name: paidAmountCurrency
fields.label: Paid Amount: Currency
fields.description: Second of item 87's two widgets. The v1.0.0 draft excluded this combobox as an "unclear" blank-default companion widget with no distinguishing printed sub-label; this cycle's corrected combobox re-extraction (see VERIFICATION.md) found it in fact carries a genuine 2-value option list (BDT/USD), so it is now modeled as the currency of the paidAmount figure.
fields.sourceRef: Page 3, item 87 companion combobox (internal AcroForm field name "840"): "Paid Amount (জমাকৃত ফি)", no printed asterisk

fields.name: declarationDate
fields.label: Date (Declaration)
fields.description: A genuine AcroForm text widget found in the page-3 declaration/signature block. Its position (roughly 70-90pt below the printed "Date ___ Signature ___" line) does not sit directly beneath either printed blank, but it is the only unclaimed fillable widget in that region of page 3, so it is modeled here as the date accompanying the applicant's/guardian's signature; disclosed as a judgment call in VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Page 3, Declaration block: "Date (তারিখ) _________________________ ... Signature of Applicant/Guardian (if Applicant is minor)"

documents.label: Applicant's declaration of accuracy
documents.statement: I solemnly declare that all the information provided in this application is true. I am aware of the rules and regulations of Passport Act. I will be subjected to said passport act/regulations for any false information or hiding of any such information.

documents.label: Photocopy of National ID
documents.sourceRef: Page 3, List of Attachments: "Photocopy of National ID (জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্রের ফটোকপি)" checkbox (internal field name "NationalID")
documents.handling: The form's own header instruction ("Please fill up the application form page 1 to 3 as per National ID/Birth Registration Certificate") implies at least one of nationalIdCopy or birthRegistrationCertificateCopy is expected, but this is disclosed in prose rather than machine-encoded, consistent with this registry's own established convention for disjunctive "at least one of" requirements the source does not itself gate with a boolean.

documents.label: Photocopy of Birth Registration Certificate
documents.sourceRef: Page 3, List of Attachments: "Photocopy of Birth Registration Certificate (জন্মনিবন্ধন সনেদের ফটোকপি)" checkbox (internal field name "BRC")
documents.handling: See nationalIdCopy's description for the disclosed "at least one of National ID or Birth Registration Certificate" business rule.

documents.label: Photocopy of Marriage Certificate
documents.sourceRef: Page 3, List of Attachments: "Photocopy of Marriage Certificate (নিকাহনামার ফটোকপি)" checkbox (internal field name "Marriage_cert")
documents.handling: Applicable when the applicant's marital status or name has changed due to marriage.

documents.label: Other
documents.sourceRef: Page 3, List of Attachments: "Others (অন্যান্য)" checkbox (internal field name "Other")
documents.handling: A catch-all checkbox for any other supporting document not covered by the checklist's other entries; the form provides no companion free-text field to specify what "Other" refers to.
